begins to commit sin and wrong and leads a sinful life with increased
longing for enjoyment. To the wealthy exist friends and retations. He
who has wealth is regarded as a man of importance by people. He is
considered a learned man. He is rvc.ky and he is intelligent. To the man
of wealth dharma and kama and all else are helpful. The man of poverty
desirous of wealth and seeking it finds it difficult to 9et."
The importance of wealth and its positive role as a contributory
force for the development of man is affirmed by the Hildu thinkers.
Money is the one indispensable m~ns for the formation and growth of
society, for the promotion and cultivation of tommerte. industry. scientifit
research and discovery. for the destruction of evil forces money is
necessary.
To tl1e Hindu an affluent society is not necessarily unspiritual.
